title: An Empirical Study on the Code Refactoring Capability of Large Language Models | ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
summary: This paper presents an empirical study evaluating the code refactoring capabilities of five Large Language Models (StarCoder2, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4o, LLaMA 3, and DeepSeek-v3) on 30 open-source Java projects. It assesses the LLMs based on their ability to improve static code quality metrics, pass unit tests, and reduce code smells. The study finds that production-grade models like GPT-4o and DeepSeek-v3 achieve high unit test success rates, while LLaMA 3 excels at code smell reduction. The authors also compare LLM performance against human developers and investigate the impact of one-shot and chain-of-thought prompting. The work provides insights into the effectiveness and best practices for integrating LLMs into software refactoring tasks.
author: Jonathan Cordeiro, Shayan Noei, Ying Zou
